title: A control arrangement for a nosepiece of a work tool, a work tool and a method for working on a workpiece.
abstract: The invention relates to a control arrangement (100) for a nosepiece (20) of a work tool (1), the control arrangement (100) comprising:an actuator device (110); and a linkage arrangement (120) connected to the actuator device (110), wherein the link-age arrangement (120) comprises a first linkage set (120') with a plurality of link elements, connected to a clamping sleeve (22) of the nosepiece (20), such that movement of the actuator device (110) in an axial direction results in a displacement of the clamping sleeve (22) in an axial direction, the first linkage set (120') comprising a first link element (122) and a second link element (124) pivotally connected to each other at a first joint (J1) forming a knee joint, the first link element (122) further being pivotally connected to a first fixed point (FP1 on the work tool (1), wherein the first joint (J1) between the first link element (122) and the second link element (124) is connected with the actuator device (110), so that a linear movement of the first joint (J1) traverse the axial direction of the actuator device (110) is allowed.
claims: Claims CLAIMS 1. A control arrangement (100) for a nosepiece (20) of a work tool (1 ), the control arrangement (100) comprising: an actuator device (110); and a linkage arrangement (120) connected to the actuator device (110), wherein the linkage arrangement (120) comprises a first linkage set (120â) with a plu- rality of link elements, connected to a clamping sleeve (22) of the nosepiece (20), such that movement of the actuator device (110) in its axial direction results in a dis- placement of the clamping sleeve (22) in its axial direction, the first linkage set (120â) comprising a first link element (122) and a second link element (124) pivotally con- nected to each other at a first joint (J1 ) forming a knee joint, the first link element (122) further being pivotally connected to a first fixed point (FP1 on the work tool (1 ), wherein the first joint (J1 ) between the first link element (122) and the second link el- ement (124) is connected with the actuator device (110), so that a linear movement of the first joint (J1 ) traverse the axial direction of the actuator device (110) is al- lowed. 2. The arrangement (100) according to claim 1 , wherein the actuator device (110) comprises a moveable rod (114) and a linear actuator (116) connected to the movea- ble rod (114), wherein the first joint (J1 ) is connected with the moveable rod (114). 3. The arrangement (100) according to claim 2, wherein the linear actuator (110) comprises a pneumatic cylinder (116) and the rod (114) is a piston rod (114) of a pis- ton moveably arranged in the cylinder (116). 4. The arrangement (100) according to claim 2 or 3, wherein the linkage arrangement (120) is configured, such that an extracted position of the moveable rod (114) corre- sponds to a first position of the clamping sleeve (22) and a retracted position of the moveable rod (114) corresponds to a second position of the clamping sleeve (22), in which second position the clamping sleeve (22) is extended. 5. The arrangement (100) according to any one of claims 2-4, wherein the first joint (J1 ) is arranged in a slot (118) in the moveable rod (114), the slot (118) allowing lin- ear movement of the first joint (J1 ) in a direction traverse the axial direction of the rod (114). 6. The arrangement (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first link element (122) and the second link element (124) are connected to a third link element (128) at the first joint (J1 ), wherein the third link element (128) is pivotally connected to the actuator device (110) to allow linear movement of the first joint (J1 ) in a direction traverse the axial direction of the actuator device (110). 7. The arrangement (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first link element (122) and the second link element (124) have similar lengths (A). 8. The arrangement (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first link element (122) and the second link element (124) forms a triangle with a base (2C) being an imaginary line extending through the first fixed point (FP1 ) and a sec- ond end (124â) of the second link element (124), wherein the height (B) of the trian- gle extends between the base (2C) and the first joint (J1 ) and the height (B) varies depending on the movement of the actuator device (110). 9. The arrangement (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the first linkage set (120â) further comprises: a fourth link element (130) having a first leverage arm (E) and a second lever- age arm (D), wherein the fourth link element (130) is pivotally connected to a second fixed point (FP2) on the work tool (1 ) and to the second link element (124). 10. The arrangement (100) according to claim 9, wherein the first linkage set (120â) further comprises: a fifth link element (132) connected to the clamping sleeve (22) and to the fourth link element (130). 11. The arrangement (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the axial direction of the actuator device (110) is parallel with the axial direction of clamping sleeve (22). 12. The arrangement (100) according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the linkage arrangement (120) comprises a second linkage set (120â) identical to the first linkage set (120â), wherein the first linkage set (120â) and the second linkage set (120â)are symmetrically arranged on opposite sides of the work tool (1 ). 13. A work tool (1 ) for working a workpiece (2), said workpiece (2) having attached thereto a template (6) with preformed guide openings (8) located in a pattern corre- sponding to the positions of holes (10) to be formed in the workpiece (2), said work tool (1 ) is provided with a nosepiece (20) comprising a clamping sleeve (22) config- ured for fixating the work tool (1 ) relative the guide openings (8) by exerting a clamp force between the nosepiece (20) and an interior wall (54) of the respective guide opening (8), the work tool (1 ) further comprising a control arrangement (100) for the nosepiece (20) according to any one of claims 1 -12. 14. A method, performed by a control device, for working a workpiece (2) by using a work tool (1 ) according to claim 13; the method comprising: controlling (s101 ) the clamp force between the nosepiece (20) and the tern- plate (6) by means of the control arrangement (100); and working (s102) the workpiece (2). 15. The method according to claim 14, further comprising, before controlling (s101 ) the clamp force: positioning (s103) the nosepiece (20) in a guide opening (8) 16. The method according to claim 15, wherein positioning (s103) the nosepiece (20) in one guide opening (8) comprises controlling a robot arm (40). 17. The method according to any one of claim 14-16, wherein controlling (s101 ) the clamp force comprises detecting an actual value of the clamp force and comparing the actual value with a desired value by a loop execution. 18. The method according to any one of claim 14-17, wherein controlling (s101 ) the clamp force comprises controlling the actuator device (110) of the control arrange- ment (100). 19. The method according to any one of claims 14-18, wherein the work tool (1 ) is a drilling machine comprising a rotary cutting tool (18), and wherein the step of working (s102) the workpiece (2) comprises a hole-cutting process performed in the work- piece (2). 20. A computer program (P) comprising instructions which, when the program is exe- cuted by a computer (44), cause the computer (44) to carry out the method according to any one of claim 14-19. 21. A computer-readable medium comprising instructions, which when executed by a computer (44), cause the computer (44) to carry out the method according to any one of claims 14-19.
